title: C4OS Grill Question 002b: Browser Trust Boundary
description: This follow-up resolves the trust boundary created by full browsing, cookies/login sessions, and file:// access. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: Which Browser context model should MVP use?
options: One isolated Browser profile per trusted project folder
One global Browser profile shared across all workspaces
Ephemeral Browser profile per session with no persistence
Separate modes: persistent web profile for web URLs, ephemeral isolated preview for local/file content
questions.help: Recommended answer: separate modes. Persistent web browsing and local/file artifact preview should not share the same trust context.

questions.label: What should file:// opening allow?
options: Only generated artifact files explicitly created by C4OS
Only files inside trusted project roots
Any local file the user chooses
Any file URL the Browser page tries to navigate to
questions.help: Recommended answer: only generated artifacts and trusted-root files, with explicit user action. Browser page content should not be able to navigate arbitrary local files.

questions.label: Can the agent control the Browser in MVP?
options: User-only Browser; agent can suggest URLs but not drive the Browser
Agent can inspect local app previews only after user approval
Agent can use Browser automation for any page in the Browser
questions.help: Your example mentions Playwright-like inspection. This needs a policy boundary separate from user browsing.

questions.label: Minimum security floor you accept
options: No privileged C4OS bridge exposed to page content
No provider credentials exposed to page content
No workspace files exposed unless trusted-root or generated artifact policy allows it
No shell state exposed to page content
Cookies/login sessions isolated by project or mode
questions.help: Select every rule that must be true for Browser to remain in MVP.

questions.label: Clarifications or constraints
questions.help: Add any details about localhost previews, external links, session persistence, artifact rendering, or user approval.
